text: fat is 15g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, this mostly lives or dies on carb behaviour.
carbs is 41g. the fibre support is doing a lot of the work. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
protein is 3.4g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
vitamin a at 0% DV, vitamin c at 6% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

pros first. strong comfort-food appeal because a very real reason people like them, even if the nutrition fit is weak. still retains some tuber identity because not nutritionally empty, just much worse than stronger tuber forms. satisfying texture and taste because part of the real-world appeal, especially for content framing.

now the downsides. fried format is a major category negative because this drags tubers away from the cleaner energy-food role the category is supposed to reward. glycemic behaviour is rough because a major practical strike against the food in a carb-behaviour category. energy density is much harsher than ideal because much easier to overshoot calories than with cleaner tuber forms.
D tier. Best only when you want easy carbs and can accept the missing upside.
